title: matricaria subpolaris
intro: Matricaria subpolaris, a type of flowering plant in the daisy family, is native to the Arctic regions of North America and Europe. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its essential oil, which contains compounds such as matricarin and chamazulene, with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. Matricaria subpolaris is used to promote digestive health, relieve stress and anxiety, and soothe skin irritations, among other health benefits. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and used as a tea, infused into oils, or added to topical creams to harness its medicinal properties.
